evals.prompt: Derive test cases for the FuelRateLimiter unit. The detailed design is in evals/files/fuel-rate-limiter-design.md. Use Python with pytest.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive pytest test file covering all behavior rules, error handling, boundary values, and equivalence classes from the design. Should include a coverage matrix.

assertions.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases and derivation strategy
assertions.grading: Check for a table or structured list mapping design elements (behavior rules, error conditions, inputs) to specific test function names with strategy labels (req-based, equivalence, boundary, error)

assertions.text: Every behavior rule (startup clamping, cruise rate-of-change, emergency shutdown, was_clamped flag) has at least one test
assertions.grading: Check test names and bodies for coverage of each behavior rule from the design

assertions.text: Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
assertions.grading: Each error condition in the design's error handling section must have its own test

assertions.text: Boundary values tested for requested_rate (0.0, at STARTUP_MIN, at STARTUP_MAX, at CRUISE_MAX, above max)
assertions.grading: Look for tests at constraint boundaries, not just mid-range values

assertions.text: Every test asserts a specific expected value — no assertNotNull, assertTrue(x), or 'does not raise' as sole assertion
assertions.grading: Read each test's assertions. Fail if any test's only assertion is assertNotNull, assertTrue without a meaningful condition, or just checking no exception is thrown

assertions.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested (input condition + expected outcome), not just the method name
assertions.grading: Test names should read like 'startup_mode_limits_rate_to_minimum' not 'test_calculate' or 'test1'

assertions.text: Stateful behavior tested: rate-of-change limiting requires multiple sequential calls
assertions.grading: At least one test makes multiple calls to verify state-dependent behavior (rate-of-change needs previous_rate)

assertions.text: Configuration variants tested: at least one test with non-default config values
assertions.grading: Check if any test constructs the unit with non-default configuration parameters

evals.prompt: I need tests for the MessageParser. Design artifact is at evals/files/message-parser-design.md. Use Java with JUnit 5.
evals.expected_output: JUnit 5 test class covering the sequential parsing steps, each error path, boundary values for raw_data length, and the union return type. Should include coverage matrix.

assertions.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases and derivation strategy
assertions.grading: Check for a table or structured list mapping design elements to specific test method names with strategy labels

assertions.text: Each sequential parsing step (B1-B10) has at least one happy-path test and its on_failure test
assertions.grading: The design lists 10 parsing steps each with an on_failure. Check that both paths are covered for key steps

assertions.text: Tests assert on the specific Success or Error variant fields, not just the top-level type
assertions.grading: Assertions should check variant-specific fields (e.g., Success.payload_length, Error.error_code) not just 'instanceof Success'

assertions.text: Boundary values for raw_data length tested: at minimum (4 bytes), below minimum, at maximum (1024), above maximum
assertions.grading: Look for tests with data at exact constraint boundaries

assertions.text: Every test asserts a specific expected value — no assertNotNull, assertTrue(x), or 'does not raise' as sole assertion
assertions.grading: Read each test's assertions. Fail if any test's only assertion is assertNotNull or assertTrue without a meaningful condition

assertions.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested (input condition + expected outcome), not just the method name
assertions.grading: Test names should read like 'rejects_data_shorter_than_header_size' not 'testParse' or 'test1'

assertions.text: Error handling entries (null/empty data, too-short data) have dedicated tests separate from parsing step failures
assertions.grading: E1 and E2 from design should have their own tests, not mixed into parsing step tests

assertions.text: Checksum verification step has both valid and invalid checksum test cases
assertions.grading: At least one test with correct checksum (passes) and one with incorrect checksum (fails with appropriate error)

evals.prompt: Write tests for the temperature controller described in evals/files/temperature-controller-design.md. Use Go with the standard testing package.
evals.expected_output: Go test file with table-driven tests covering hysteresis/deadband behavior, state transitions, error cases (out-of-range inputs, clamping), and boundary values. Should include coverage matrix.

assertions.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases and derivation strategy
assertions.grading: Check for a table or structured list mapping design elements to specific test names with strategy labels

assertions.text: All state transitions tested: IDLE->HEAT, IDLE->COOL, HEAT->IDLE, COOL->IDLE, and no-change cases
assertions.grading: Check for tests covering each transition direction and steady-state (remain in current state)

assertions.text: Deadband boundary values tested: exactly at target±deadband/2 threshold and just inside/outside
assertions.grading: Tests should probe the exact hysteresis thresholds, not just clearly-hot and clearly-cold values

assertions.text: Out-of-range input handling tested: temperature outside sensor range, target outside valid range, zero/negative deadband
assertions.grading: Each error case from the design should have a test verifying the clamping or error behavior

assertions.text: Every test asserts specific expected values for both action (HEAT/COOL/IDLE) and stateChanged flag
assertions.grading: Assertions must check both output fields with exact expected values, not just one of them

assertions.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
assertions.grading: For Go: t.Run names should be descriptive like 'below_lower_threshold_triggers_heat' not 'case1'

assertions.text: First-call behavior tested: initial state with no prior history
assertions.grading: At least one test verifies behavior on the very first call (no previous state)

assertions.text: Tests verify behavior with both default configuration and non-default values
assertions.grading: At least one test uses non-default target, deadband, or sensor range values

evals.prompt: Here's a Layer 1 component design for a session manager: evals/files/session-manager-design-L1.md. It has explicit dependencies (Clock, TokenGenerator, SessionRepository). Derive test cases that exercise the component's contracts, constraints, and error handling. Use Python with pytest.
evals.expected_output: Test cases covering external interface contracts, dependency injection with appropriate test doubles, constraint validation (10k sessions, timing, TTL), error strategy (result types), and threading considerations. Coverage matrix mapping to component-level elements.

assertions.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping component-level design elements to test cases and derivation strategy
assertions.grading: Check for a table or structured list mapping design elements (interfaces, constraints, error variants, dependencies) to test names with strategy labels

assertions.text: Clock dependency is injected and controlled in tests — not using real system time
assertions.grading: Tests should inject a fake/stub clock to control time for expiry testing. Fail if tests use time.sleep() or real system clock for TTL checks

assertions.text: TokenGenerator is injected with a deterministic substitute for predictable test assertions
assertions.grading: Tests should use a fake or stub TokenGenerator that returns known values, enabling specific assertions on token values

assertions.text: SessionRepository uses a real in-memory implementation, not a mock
assertions.grading: The default in-memory implementation should be used directly (it's a simple data structure, not infrastructure). Fail if SessionRepository is mocked with unittest.mock or similar

assertions.text: Tests assert on specific SessionResult variants (Success with SessionInfo fields, or Error with specific SessionError enum value)
assertions.grading: Assertions should check the variant type AND its contents (e.g., error == SessionError.expired, not just 'is error')

assertions.text: Every test asserts a specific expected value — no assertNotNull, assertTrue(x), or 'does not raise' as sole assertion
assertions.grading: Read each test's assertions. Fail if any test's only assertion is asserting not-None or just checking no exception

assertions.text: Capacity constraint tested: behavior at 10,000 sessions (at limit) and 10,001 (over limit, should get store_full error)
assertions.grading: Look for boundary tests at the max concurrent sessions limit

assertions.text: TTL expiry tested by advancing the fake clock past 30 minutes, then validating the session returns 'expired'
assertions.grading: At least one test creates a session, advances clock past TTL, and verifies validation returns expired error
